expectations.text: Sets out the timeline correctly: confirm receipt within 10 business days; substantive response within 45 calendar days, extendable by a further 45 days with notice
expectations.evidence: Response states 'Log the date/time of receipt for each request type — this starts the clock on your 45-calendar-day response deadline (extendable once by another 45 days...)' but never mentions the 10-business-day acknowledgment/receipt-confirmation requirement anywhere in the response. Only the 45-day substantive deadline is covered.

expectations.text: Explains tiered identity verification (reasonable degree vs reasonably high degree depending on sensitivity; specific-pieces requests require higher assurance) and that unverifiable delete requests may be treated as opt-out where applicable
expectations.evidence: Mentions '2 data points' standard vs '3 data points plus signed declaration' for sensitive/financial requests, which captures some tiering, but never frames this as 'reasonable degree' vs 'reasonably high degree' tied to specific-pieces requests, and does not mention that an unverifiable delete request may be treated as an opt-out.

expectations.text: Right to know: covers categories AND specific pieces, sources, purposes, third parties — with the 12-month lookback default (and the CPRA extension beyond 12 months where feasible on request)
expectations.evidence: Covers specific pieces, categories, sources, purposes, third parties — but on the lookback period it states: 'the 12-month lookback restriction that used to apply to Right to Know has been effectively eliminated for PI collected after January 1, 2022 under CPRA — so... you should generally provide full history since that date, not just the trailing 12 months.' This is an inaccurate/fabricated characterization contradicting the assertion's requirement of a 12-month default with a feasible extension; the response asserts the opposite (no default, full history required).

expectations.text: Deletion: instructs flowing the deletion to service providers/contractors, and cites legitimate statutory exceptions (e.g., complete the transaction, security/fraud, legal compliance, internal uses compatible with expectations) rather than inventing exceptions
expectations.evidence: Lists 7 exceptions matching the statutory list (complete transaction, security/fraud, debug, free speech, legal compliance §1798.145(a), internal purposes compatible with context, research/journalism) and instructs to 'send deletion instructions to every service provider and contractor holding a copy.'

expectations.text: Advises maintaining request records (24-month record-keeping) and responding even when denying (state the reason and the exception relied on)
expectations.evidence: 'If your business processes PI for 10 million or more consumers/households, retain records... for 24 months' and Step 4 instructs to 'Notify the consumer within 45 days that deletion is complete — or, for any PI retained under an exception, explain which exception was invoked and why.'
